english: the matter; gsó-mkan ma byuṅ dógs-nas (gso mkhan ma byung dogs nas) Glr. fearing that no deliverer would make his appearance; hence for that not, lest and similar expressions, bu mis mtóṅ-gis dógs nas (bu mis mthong gis dogs nas) that his son might not be seen by the people Pth.; że-sdaṅ laṅs dogs túr-re gyis (zhe sdang langs dogs thur re gyis) be on your guard lest anger should arise, take care not to grow angry! Mil.; ₒgos dógs-pai lčibs ('gos dogs pa'i lcibs) dusters to prevent (things) from getting dirty Lex.; gżaṅ-gyis gsál-bar śes-kyis dogs(-na) (gzhan gyis gsal bar shes kyis dogs (-na)) using distant allusions, so that the drift of a speech is not at once clear and intelligible Gram.; rarely with the supine: dé - dag bág - tu (de dag bag tu), or ₒbrós - su dogs ('bros su dogs) fearing lest they should become faint-hearted or take to flight Dzl. — 2. sbst. apprehension, fear, scruple, dógs-pa skyes-te (dogs pa skyes te) Dzl.; also dogs skyés-te (dogs skyes te) Glr.; dogs bsál-ba (dogs bsal ba), dogs gčód-pa (dogs gcod pa) to remove doubts or apprehensions Tar.; dogs dpyod ni dogs gčód - do (dogs dpyod ni dogs gcod do) examining a scruple is as much as removing it Sch.; re-dógs (re dogs) hope and fear (things which a saint ought to be no longer subject to) frq.

1. a deep hole, pit, ditch, an excavation deep in proportion to its breadth, e.g. a trench in fortifications, Glr.; sa-doṅ (sa dong) id.; ču-doṅ (chu dong) a well, a deep cistern; me - doṅ (me dong) a fiery abyss, pool of fire Dzl.; Sch. proposes to use it also for crater. — 2. depth, deepness, profundity; dóṅ-čan (dong can) Cs., *dóṅ - po* (*dong po*) W., deep; doṅ - méd (dong med) not deep, shallow Cs. — 3. v. ₒdóṅ-ba ('dong ba).
n. of a tropical climbing plant, and of a sweet-tasted lenient purgative Med.
padlock, dóṅ-pa ₒjùg-pa (dong pa 'jug pa) to put a padlock on.

1. tube, any hollow cylindrical vessel, = pu-ri (pu ri); dóṅ-bu (dong bu) a small ditto; spa-dóṅ (spa dong) a tube etc. of bamboo, śiṅ-doṅ (shing dong) a tube etc. of wood; lčags-doṅ (lcags dong) of iron; mda-doṅ (mda' dong) a quiver, dóṅ-ba (dong ba) Glr. id.; dóṅ-mo (dong mo), ldóṅ-mo (ldong mo) a small churn, = gur-gúr (gur gur). — 2 a shuttle, made of a piece of bamboo.
Sch. also dóṅ-tse (dong tse), dóṅ-rtse (dong rtse), piece of money, coin, gsér-gyi (gser gyi) gold coin Dzl.; esp. a small coin, used (like penny) proverbially for a small sum, Dzl. 222, 9; 222, 6.

an equivalent, *ṅul méd-na dod čig ₒtob gos* (*ngul med na dod cig 'thob gos*) W. if you have no money, I must receive an equivalent; dei dod či-ₒdra yod (de'i dod ci 'dra yod) what is the equivalent, what shall we get for it? Mil.; bu-dód (bu dod) adoptive son, ṅéd-kyi bu-dód mdzod (nged kyi bu dod mdzod) pray, suffer yourself to be adopted by us Mil.; skad - dód (skad dod) verbal equivalent, synonym, translation Lex.; dód-du (dod du) as an equivalent, as payment, for, instead of, at, e.g. at a moderate price; kyód-kyis ṅai stóbs-kyi dod mi per (khyod kyis nga'i stobs kyi dod mi pher) Glr., gen. *mi nuṅ* (*mi nung*) C., you cannot cope with me in strength, you are no match for me.
to project, to be prominent, gen. with ₒbur - du ('bur du); also elongated (Botany) Wdn.

(Ssk. अर्थ), resp. (at least in some of its applications) żabs-don (zhabs don) Pth. 1. sense, meaning, signification, gó-ba (go ba) to understand, ₒgrél-ba ('grel ba) to explain; don rnyéd-par dká-bai yig - ₒbrú (don rnyed par dka' ba'i yig 'bru) letters the meaning of which is not easily understood Glr ; don mi ₒdug (don mi 'dug) that makes no sense; ₒdii don či yin ('di'i don ci yin) what does that mean? żal ni k'ai don yin (zhal ni kha'i don yin): 'żal' ('zhal') signifies the same as k'a (kha); dpe bżi don daṅ lṅai mgur (dpe bzhi don dang lnga'i mgur) a psalm, containing four parables, together with their explanation, as being the fifth (part) Mil.; ráṅ-gi-séms-la don gyis (rang gi sems la don gyis) refer the signification, make the application, to your own soul Mil.; .. kyi dón-du bśad (kyi don du bshad), it is explained in the sense of . ., as having the same meaning as . . Gram.; don mtún-no (don mthun no) they agree in this sense, on that point, they say so unanimously Glr.; don dé-la soms (don de la soms) think over this sense, i.e. over the meaning of this significant example Mil.; żu-dón (zhu don) application, petition, request; contents, Tar. 45, 19.; also opp. to tsig (tshig) (word, form); čos-byuṅ-na spri-ti-ma zer-ba ₒdug-ste don mtun (chos byung na spri ti ma zer ba 'dug ste don mthun) in the čos - byuṅ (chos byung), it is true, he is called Spritima, but the contents (i.e.
